title: festuca gautieri subsp. lutea
intro: Festuca gautieri subsp. lutea is a subspecies of the Festuca gautieri plant, a type of grass in the family Poaceae. It is also known as yellow fescue, which is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Festuca gautieri subsp. lutea can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
